parsed_review.summary: CP00-671 (RP22 External Integrations I, permission/audit fixture bridge v2; risk B, 40 units, RP22.P02.M04.S19-RP22.P02.M06.S14) is a descriptor-only slice and its production_ready promotion is well supported. Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only. (1) Scope consistency: the deliverable distribution {test:8,contract:2,implementation:17,security_audit:4,ui:5,claude_review:2,failure_recovery:2} and micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:4,Permission And Audit Binding:22,Synthetic Fixture Set:14} each total 40 and match exactly across registry.js EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP671_REQUIREMENTS, the review baseline included_units, the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and both contract files. (2) Implementation: service.js builds the CP671 case-set/descriptor/Hermes/Claude/handoff with all runtimes closed; validators.js enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included=false, deny cross-tenant, persistence no-write, and claude_is_final_approval=false; the contract projection advances current_pack/generated_from_pack_id to CP00-671 with CP666-670 as history; index.js re-exports the full surface. (3) Tests: model.test.js asserts CP671 coverage, descriptor boundaries (permission/audit/fixture closed, external_api_runtime_opened=false, handoff to CP00-672/RP22.P02.M06.S15), and projection equality against both contract files. (4) No-write/no-real-data: freezeCp671Result + CP666_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION assert synthetic_only/no_real_data and no token/secret/credential persistence; a secret scan of the implementation surface returned only boolean *:false flags, no real values. (5) Authority/Hermes boundary: Claude stays read_only and non-final (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2=true, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob); Hermes is used only as an H22 evidence-packet descriptor (hermes_runtime_opened=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, runtime_receipt_emitted=false), not product runtime. (6) Honesty of claims: manifest/command-evidence/construction-inspection report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status pending_pack_level_review; readiness auth preflight passed with well-formed non-empty JSON. command-evidence reports the full validation matrix (closeout-pack/plan validators, rp22 validators, model tests, weighted/spec/fullplan/global/goal/RP00 validators, git diff --check, npm test, build) at exit 0 — these are pack-reported and not re-run by this read-only reviewer. The pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust, so this CP closeout goal is not blocked. Only one cosmetic, non-blocking scope-file classification note found; no P0/P1/P2 issues.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists new-session-handoff.md as both untracked implementation and unrelated dirty file
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 184) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 192), while correctly being absent from active_pack_required_untracked_files. This is a benign redundancy in generated tooling metadata: the file is a session-handoff doc, not a CP00-671 artifact, and is correctly excluded from the pack's required files. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no in-scope file was hidden from review, and the manifest classifies the doc only under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No effect on scope correctness, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, validation, or the production_ready claim.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the scope-generator so a file classified as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is not also emitted under untracked_implementation_files, to avoid future reader confusion. Non-blocking; no action required for CP00-671 closeout.
